results.description: per-file function/class counts as a table
results.intent: for each python file in the project, count functions and classes, print a table
results.program:
files = find_files('src/**/*.py')
data = []
for f in files:
    funcs = len(find_names(f, '.fn'))
    classes = len(find_names(f, '.class'))
    data.append((f, funcs, classes))
print(tabulate(data, headers=['File', 'Functions', 'Classes']))
results.raw:
files = find_files('src/**/*.py')
data = []
for f in files:
    funcs = len(find_names(f, '.fn'))
    classes = len(find_names(f, '.class'))
    data.append((f, funcs, classes))
print(tabulate(data, headers=['File', 'Functions', 'Classes']))

results.description: iterate classes, list methods per class
results.intent: find all classes, then for each class show its methods
results.program:
find_names('src/**/*.py', '.class')
for cls in result:
    methods = find_names('src/**/*.py', f'.class#{cls} .fn')
    print(f'Class {cls}: {methods}')
results.raw:

    print(f'Class {cls}: {methods}')

results.intent: find the 3 most complex functions and show their source code
results.raw:
complexity('src/**/*.py', '.fn', top=3)
show me all imports in cli.py -> read_source('squackit/cli.py', 'imports')
what is the dependency graph of my project? -> dependency_graph()
find all instances of deprecated functions -> search('deprecated')

results.description: set difference between src and test function names
results.intent: compare the function names in tests/ vs src/ to find untested functions
results.program:
test_functions = find_names('tests/**/*.py', '.fn')
src_functions = find_names('src/**/*.py', '.fn')
untested_functions = [func for func in src_functions if func not in test_functions]
print(untested_functions)
results.raw:
test_functions = find_names('tests/**/*.py', '.fn')
src_functions = find_names('src/**/*.py', '.fn')
untested_functions = [func for func in src_functions if func not in test_functions]
print(untested_functions)
find all function calls to validate_token -> source = "src/**/*.py"
selector = ".fn .call[name='validate_token']"
result = find_calls(source, selector)
result
find the most common imports -> analyze_imports('src/**/*.py')
show me a graph of dependencies -> dependency_graph('src/**/*.py')

results.description: per-file function count with filtering and sorting
results.intent: find all files that have more than 10 functions and rank them
show me a summary: how many functions, classes, and files, plus the top 5 most complex
find all classes that have an __init__ method and show what parameters it takes
results.raw:
find_names('src/**/*.py', '.class[name*="__init__"] .fn')
find functions with a specific docstring pattern -> find_names('src/**/*.py', '.fn[doc*="TODO"]')
find all classes that inherit from BaseModel -> find_names('src/**/*.py', '.class[inherits*="BaseModel"]')
show me the inheritance hierarchy of the Auth class -> investigate('.class#Auth')
find functions with a specific return type -> find_names('src/**/*.py', '.fn[return*="int"]')
find all classes that have at least one method with a specific decorator -> find_names('src/**/*.py', '.class[methods*="@decorator"]')
show me the dependencies of cli.py -> dependency_graph('cli.py')
what are the hotspots in terms of complexity and cyclomatic complexity -> hotspot_analysis()
find all functions that call another function recursively -> find_r

results.intent: for each module, find functions that start with _ (private) and list them grouped by file
results.raw:
private_functions = {}
modules = ['cli', 'server', 'auth']
for module in modules:
    files = glob.glob(f'src/{module}/*.py')
    for file in files:
        functions = find_names(file, '.fn[name^="_"]')
        if functions:
            private_functions[file] = functions
for file, funcs in private_functions.items():
    print(f"{file}: {funcs}")
find all function calls to validate_token -> source = "src/**/*.py"
selector = ".fn .call#validate_token"
result = find_calls(source, selector)
result
show me the most common imports -> analyze_imports()
find all instances of deprecated code -> deprecated('src/**/*.py')
find functions with no docstring -> undocumented('src/**/*.py', '.fn')
check for magic numbers -> check_magic_numbers('src/**/*.py')
